column1: Storm Surge updates for Sunday afternoon and evening. We'll be updating graphics and information all night long and I'll have a DPL starting at 7pm. Link will be posted. LIVE update on ABC Action News at 6.
You are the only weather person I watch..Been following you you for over 20 yrs..You are simply the best,, As Sheriff Judd would put it... Lol
We’re in Destin for my hsubands work temporarily do we have anything we worry about??? I’ve been freaking out
don't freak out it's only a storm
Ugh, I work on the water in Crystal River I hope it's not going to get flooded again! Last year we were closed for a month!
I would prepare for that possibility
ya, prepare to be broke again🙄 Nothing else I can really do.
Port Richey this afternoon
great Photo
wow! Neat picture 😮🙌🏼
it was not TS related. I live in port Richey too and it did get squally for a little bit.
Dunedin Causeway right now
Noticed that high tide measurements in the bay have been 1/2+ ft higher the past couple of days without this storm so 2 - 4 should concern some.
Yeah I was wondering if king tides were going to get mentioned. That's what screwed people up on the barrier island for TS Eta and Idalia.
Redington Beach 6pm

On this storm, I wouldn’t get too shook up unless something dramatic changes.

white is 2-4 and pink is 3-5

If there is a 2-4’ surge, how does that compare to previous surges like the no name storm in December or Idelia . . . ?

Idalia was a super moon at LOW tide and we flooded. If I recall, the surge was projected to be 4-6' 🫤 Keep in mind it was a Cat 3 and running much closer to the shoreline. Best of luck to you, stay dry.
add it to you tide at the time. This passed us further south right at our high tide. Everyone has different tide charts to consult.
